questions.question: Choose the article for one countable noun: I need {a1} chair.
Choose a natural quantifier for uncountable noun: I need {a1} water.
Choose the uncountable noun form: This {a1} is useful.
Choose the quantifier for plural countable noun: How {a1} books do you have?
Choose the quantifier for uncountable noun: We have very {a1} time.
Choose the positive small amount for plural countable noun: I have {a1} friends here.
Choose the quantifier in a negative sentence: I do not have {a1} questions.
Choose the quantifier that works here: She has {a1} experience.
Choose the partitive: I need {a1} advice.
Choose the correct uncountable form: The teacher gave us {a1}.
Avoid article mistake with uncountable noun: She gave me good {a1}.
Choose the correct uncountable noun form: Her {a1} is heavy.
